at-limit

short-names
unnamed-constraint

invalid.name: column-name

errors.message: Identifier `aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa` is 64 bytes, which exceeds the 63-byte limit. PostgreSQL silently truncates over-length identifiers at parse time, so the object will be created (or looked up) under a different name than written — every later `DROP` / `ALTER` / `\d` that uses the original name will then fail with `does not exist`.
